efs.mounttarget.CfnAbout: The `AWS::EFS::MountTarget` resource is an Amazon EFS resource that creates a mount target for an EFS file system. You can then mount the file system on Amazon EC2 instances or other resources by using the mount target.

efs.mounttarget.FileSystemId.Description: The ID of the file system for which to create the mount target.

efs.mounttarget.IpAddress.Description: Valid IPv4 address within the address range of the specified subnet.

efs.mounttarget.SecurityGroups.Description: Up to five VPC security group IDs, of the form `sg-xxxxxxxx`. These must be for the same VPC as subnet specified.

efs.mounttarget.SubnetId.Description: The ID of the subnet to add the mount target in. For file systems that use One Zone storage classes, use the subnet that is associated with the file system's Availability Zone.
